posts.text: Black-owned employer businesses surpassed 200,000 for the first time in 2023—up 62% since 2017. But growth is slowing and unevenly distributed across cities and industries. New research from Andre Perry and Hannah Stephens examines what policy can do to sustain the momentum. brook.gs/4drtkfa

posts.text: The IRS has lost 27% of its staff and gone through 7 commissioners in the past year. Vanessa Williamson and Ellis Chen break down what this means for taxpayers this filing season—and why the long-term risks go well beyond delayed refunds. brook.gs/4rxlt33
posts.quote: The IRS has lost 27% of its staff and gone through 7 commissioners in the past year. Vanessa Williamson and Ellis Chen break down what this means for taxpayers this filing season—and why the long-term risks go well beyond delayed refunds. brook.gs/4rxlt33

posts.text: USPS has hit its $15B borrowing limit and holds about one month of cash. Elena Patel walks through the structural mismatch between the universal service mandate and how it's financed—and what Congress could do about it. brook.gs/4soPvak
posts.quote: USPS has hit its $15B borrowing limit and holds about one month of cash. Elena Patel walks through the structural mismatch between the universal service mandate and how it's financed—and what Congress could do about it. brook.gs/4soPvak

posts.text: As federal gridlock persists, states are diverging into two distinct governance models. From abortion to elections, Elaine Kamarck argues that the "laboratories of democracy" are becoming a partisan patchwork, offering a national safety valve, but at a high cost to unified rights. brook.gs/4b3vu34

posts.text: New research from Annelies Goger finds that, despite support for expanding apprenticeships in the U.S., core obstacles remain. "To scale participation, policymakers need to make structural changes—lowering the barriers without sacrificing rigor and quality," she writes. brook.gs/47fJR1O

posts.text: Could Texas flip the Senate? After Talarico's primary win and Cornyn's near-upset, Brookings' Bill Galston breaks down why the Lone Star State's May GOP runoff may help decide who controls the Senate in 2027. brook.gs/47fRTaY
posts.quote: Could Texas flip the Senate? After Talarico's primary win and Cornyn's near-upset, Brookings' Bill Galston breaks down why the Lone Star State's May GOP runoff may help decide who controls the Senate in 2027. brook.gs/47fRTaY

posts.text: 2026’s joint review of the USMCA will shape the future of North American trade and competitiveness. The USMCA Forward 2026 report brings together expert analysis on what is working in the agreement—and where reforms are needed across key sectors and labor. brook.gs/4aMriog
